Guide to AI for Resumes, Cover Letters, and Career Growth

  • Share This: Share Guide to AI for Resumes, Cover Letters, and Career Growth on FacebookShare Guide to AI for Resumes, Cover Letters, and Career Growth on LinkedInShare Guide to AI for Resumes, Cover Letters, and Career Growth on X

Use the tools. Keep your voice. Avoid workslop.

AI is everywhere now. It’s built into Microsoft Word, running in your browser, and showing up in LinkedIn. It’s fast and helpful. It also makes it very easy to turn in workslop: …

  • Share This: Share Nominate a WSU student for the Student Employee of the Year Award! on FacebookShare Nominate a WSU student for the Student Employee of the Year Award! on LinkedInShare Nominate a WSU student for the Student Employee of the Year Award! on X

At Washington State University, student employees are more than just help around the office—they’re innovators, leaders, collaborators, and community builders. 16 things computer science and IT majors should do in December to help their 2026 job search

  • Share This: Share 16 things computer science and IT majors should do in December to help their 2026 job search on FacebookShare 16 things computer science and IT majors should do in December to help their 2026 job search on LinkedInShare 16 things computer science and IT majors should do in December to help their 2026 job search on X

For computer science and IT majors, December is a decisive month. While others might slow down for the holidays, those who take advantage of this window can dramatically strengthen their 2026 job search. Recruiters and hiring managers consistently emphasize that …

Holiday Tie-Drive

  • Share This: Share Holiday Tie-Drive on FacebookShare Holiday Tie-Drive on LinkedInShare Holiday Tie-Drive on X

It’s the most wonderful time of the year… WSU ASCC’s annual Holiday Tie-Drive! Help us reach our goal of 125 ties before the Spring Career Expo! Every donation goes to the Cougar Closet – a free professional resource to students in need of professional wear for occasions such as interviews, internships, and everything in-between!

By Nicole Billings
Nicole Billings Student Employment Coordinator

Labor Market InsightsIn partnership with

Explore occupations by career categories and pathways and use real time labor market data to power your decision making.

Find career data by selecting keywords Keyword Search
or, by filtering for industry and occupation Industry Search

First, choose an industry of interest, then filter for occupation. (If you'd like to see data for a specific location only, filter by state.)

  • 01 Occupation Description
  • 02 Employment Trends
  • 03 Top Employers
  • 04 Education Levels
  • 05 Annual Earnings
  • 06 Technical Skills
  • 07 Core Competencies
  • 08 Job Titles

Employment Trends

The number of jobs in the career for the past two years, the current year, and projections for the next 10 years. Job counts include both employed and self-employed persons, and do not distinguish between full- and part-time jobs. Sources include Emsi industry data, staffing patterns, and OES data.

Top Employers

These companies are currently hiring for .

    Education Levels

    The educational attainment percentage breakdown for a career (e.g. the percentage of people in the career who hold Bachelor’s Degrees vs. Associate Degrees). Educational attainment levels are provided by O*NET.

    Annual Earnings

    Earnings figures are based on OES data from the BLS and include base rate, cost of living allowances, guaranteed pay, hazardous-duty pay, incentive pay (including commissions and bonuses), on-call pay, and tips.

    Technical Skills

    A list of hard skills associated with a given career ordered by the number of unique job postings which ask for those skills.

      Core Competencies

      The skills for the career. The "importance" is how relevant the ability is to the occupation: scale of 1-5. The "level" is the proficiency required by the occupation: scale of 0-100. Results are sorted by importance first, then level.

        Job Titles

        A list of job titles for all unique postings in a given career, sorted by frequency.
